verses.book: The Paschal Canon of Anatolius of Alexandria.
verses.text: I am aware that very many other matters were discussed by them, some of them with considerable probability, and others of them as matters of the clearest demonstration, by which they endeavour to prove that the festival of the Passover and unleavened bread ought by all means to be kept after the equinox.

verses.book: The Paschal Canon of Anatolius of Alexandria.
verses.text: But I shall pass on without demanding such copious demonstrations (on subjects) from which the veil of the Mosaic law has been removed; for now it remains for us with unveiled face to behold ever as in a glass Christ Himself and the doctrines and sufferings of Christ.

verses.book: The Paschal Canon of Anatolius of Alexandria.
verses.text: But that the first month among the Hebrews is about the equinox, is clearly shown also by what is taught in the book of Enoch.
